operations.description:
List customers.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

params.name: query
params.description: Filter by name, email, or external ID.

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

params.name: sorting
params.description: Sorting criterion. Several criteria can be used simultaneously and will be applied in order. Add a minus sign `-` before the criteria name to sort by descending order.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Filter by metadata key-value pairs using ``deepObject`` style, e.g. ``?metadata[key]=value``.

operations.description:
Create a customer.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

body.name: metadata
body.description:
Custom key-value attributes.

Keys: string, max **40** chars.  Values: string (max **500** chars), integer, float, or boolean.  Limit: **50** pairs.

body.name: external_id
body.description: The ID of the customer in your system. This must be unique within the workspace. Once set, it can't be updated.

body.name: email
body.description: The email address of the customer. This must be unique within the workspace.

body.name: type
body.description: The type of customer. Defaults to 'individual'. Set to 'team' for customers that can have multiple members.

body.name: workspace_id
body.description: The ID of the workspace owning the customer. **Required unless you use an workspace token.**

body.name: owner
body.description: Optional owner member to create with the customer. If not provided, an owner member will be automatically created using the customer's email and name.

operations.description:
Export customers as a CSV file.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

Get a customer by ID.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

Update a customer.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

body.name: metadata
body.description:
Custom key-value attributes.

Keys: string, max **40** chars.  Values: string (max **500** chars), integer, float, or boolean.  Limit: **50** pairs.

body.name: email
body.description: The email address of the customer. This must be unique within the workspace.

body.name: external_id
body.description: The ID of the customer in your system. This must be unique within the workspace. Once set, it can't be updated.

body.name: type
body.description: The customer type. Can only be upgraded from 'individual' to 'team', never downgraded.

operations.description:
Delete a customer.

This action cannot be undone and will immediately:
- Soft-delete the customer record
- Clear any `external_id`

Set `anonymize=true` to also anonymize PII for GDPR compliance.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

params.name: anonymize
params.description: If true, also anonymize the customer's personal data for GDPR compliance. This replaces email with a hashed version, hashes name and billing name, clears billing address, and removes OAuth account data.

operations.description:
Get a customer by external ID.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

Update a customer by external ID.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

body.name: metadata
body.description:
Custom key-value attributes.

Keys: string, max **40** chars.  Values: string (max **500** chars), integer, float, or boolean.  Limit: **50** pairs.

body.name: email
body.description: The email address of the customer. This must be unique within the workspace.

operations.description:
Delete a customer by external ID.

Soft-deletes the customer record.

Set `anonymize=true` to also anonymize PII for GDPR compliance.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

params.name: anonymize
params.description: If true, also anonymize the customer's personal data for GDPR compliance.

operations.description:
Get a customer state by ID.

The customer state includes information about
the customer's active meters.

It's the ideal endpoint to use when you need to get a full overview
of a customer's status.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

operations.summary: Get Customer State by External ID
operations.description:
Get a customer state by external ID.

The customer state includes information about
the customer's active meters.

It's the ideal endpoint to use when you need to get a full overview
of a customer's status.

**Scopes**: `customers:read` `customers:write`

List products.

**Scopes**: `shares:read` `shares:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

params.name: sorting
params.description: Sorting criterion. Several criteria can be used simultaneously and will be applied in order. Add a minus sign `-` before the criteria name to sort by descending order.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Filter by metadata key-value pairs using ``deepObject`` style, e.g. ``?metadata[key]=value``.

operations.description:
Create a share.

**Scopes**: `shares:write`

body.name: metadata
body.description:
Custom key-value attributes.

Keys: string, max **40** chars.  Values: string (max **500** chars), integer, float, or boolean.  Limit: **50** pairs.

body.name: visibility
body.description: Publishing lifecycle state for storefront exposure.

body.name: prices
body.description: List of available prices for this share. It should contain at most one static price (fixed, custom or free).

body.name: medias
body.description: List of file IDs. Each one must be on the same workspace as the share, of type `product_media` and correctly uploaded.

body.name: attached_custom_fields
body.description: List of custom fields to attach.

body.name: workspace_id
body.description: The ID of the workspace owning the share. **Required unless you use an workspace token.**

operations.description:
Get a share by ID.

**Scopes**: `shares:read` `shares:write`

Update a share.

**Scopes**: `shares:write`

body.name: metadata
body.description:
Custom key-value attributes.

Keys: string, max **40** chars.  Values: string (max **500** chars), integer, float, or boolean.  Limit: **50** pairs.

body.name: is_archived
body.description: Whether the share is archived. If `true`, the share won't be available for purchase anymore.

body.name: prices
body.description: List of available prices for this share. If you want to keep existing prices, include them in the list as an `ExistingSharePrice` object.

body.name: medias
body.description: List of file IDs. Each one must be on the same workspace as the share, of type `product_media` and correctly uploaded.

operations.description:
Initiate a file upload by requesting pre-signed URLs.

**Scopes**: `files:write`

List files.

**Scopes**: `files:read` `files:write`

params.name: name
params.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the file name. SQL ``%`` and ``_`` wildcards in the input are escaped.

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

operations.description:
Mark a file upload as completed.

**Scopes**: `files:write`

Update a file.

**Scopes**: `files:write`

Delete a file.

**Scopes**: `files:write`

Ingest batch of events.

**Scopes**: `events:write`

Get an event by ID.

**Scopes**: `events:read` `events:write`

List events.

**Scopes**: `events:read` `events:write`

params.name: parent_id
params.description: When combined with depth, use this event as the anchor instead of root events.

params.name: depth
params.description: Fetch descendants up to this depth. When set: 0=root events only, 1=roots+children, etc. Max 5. When not set, returns all events.

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

params.name: sorting
params.description: Sorting criterion. Several criteria can be used simultaneously and will be applied in order. Add a minus sign `-` before the criteria name to sort by descending order.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Filter by metadata key-value pairs using ``deepObject`` style, e.g. ``?metadata[key]=value``.

operations.description:
List event names.

**Scopes**: `events:read` `events:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

params.name: sorting
params.description: Sorting criterion. Several criteria can be used simultaneously and will be applied in order. Add a minus sign `-` before the criteria name to sort by descending order.

operations.description:
Get aggregate statistics grouped by root event name over time.

Returns time series data with periods and totals, similar to the metrics endpoint.
Each period contains stats grouped by event name, and totals show overall stats
across all periods.

**Scopes**: `events:read` `events:write`

params.name: timezone
params.description: Timezone to use for the dates. Default is UTC.

params.name: aggregate_fields
params.description: Metadata field paths to aggregate (e.g., '_cost.amount', 'duration_ns'). Use dot notation for nested fields.

params.name: sorting
params.description: Sorting criterion. Several criteria can be used simultaneously and will be applied in order. Add a minus sign `-` before the criteria name to sort by descending order.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Filter by metadata key-value pairs using ``deepObject`` style, e.g. ``?metadata[key]=value``.

operations.description:
Return the allowed day-ranges for each time interval.

**Scopes**: `metrics:read`

Get metrics about your file shares and costs.

Currency values are output in cents.

**Scopes**: `metrics:read`

params.name: timezone
params.description: Timezone to use for the timestamps. Default is UTC.

params.name: metrics
params.description: List of metric slugs to focus on. When provided, only the queries needed for these metrics will be executed, improving performance. If not provided, all metrics are returned.

operations.description:
Schedule the re-delivery of a webhook event.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:write`

List webhook deliveries.

Deliveries are all the attempts to deliver a webhook event to an endpoint.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:read` `webhooks:write`

params.name: http_code_class
params.description: Filter by HTTP response code class (2xx, 3xx, 4xx, 5xx).

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

operations.description:
List webhook endpoints.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:read` `webhooks:write`

params.name: url
params.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the webhook endpoint URL. SQL ``%`` and ``_`` wildcards in the input are escaped.

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

operations.description:
Create a webhook endpoint.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:write`

body.name: url
body.description: The URL where the webhook events will be sent.

body.name: format
body.description: Serialisation format for outgoing webhook payloads.

body.name: events
body.description: The events that will trigger the webhook.

body.name: workspace_id
body.description: The workspace ID associated with the webhook endpoint. **Required unless you use an workspace token.**

operations.description:
Get a webhook endpoint by ID.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:read` `webhooks:write`

Update a webhook endpoint.

**Scopes**: `webhooks:write`

body.name: enabled
body.description: Whether the webhook endpoint is enabled.

operations.description:
Delete a webhook endpoint.

Regenerate a webhook endpoint secret.

Create an workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

body.name: details
body.description: Additional, private, business details Rapidly needs about active workspaces for compliance (KYC).

**Scopes**: `workspaces:read` `workspaces:write`

body.name: details
body.description: Additional, private, business details Rapidly needs about active workspaces for compliance (KYC).

body.name: default_presentment_currency
body.description: Default presentment currency for the workspace

operations.description:
Request deletion of a workspace.

If the workspace has no blocking resources, it will be immediately
soft-deleted. If it has a Stripe account, that will be deleted first.

If deletion cannot proceed immediately (e.g. Stripe deletion fails),
a support ticket will be created for manual handling.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

Get the account for an workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:read` `workspaces:write`

Switch the workspace's active Stripe account to an existing one.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`
Get payment status and onboarding steps for an workspace.

params.name: account_verification_only
params.description: Only perform account verification checks, skip share and integration checks

operations.description:
List members in an workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:read` `workspaces:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

operations.description:
Invite a user to join an workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

body.name: email
body.description: Email address of the user to invite

operations.description:
Leave an workspace.

Users can only leave an workspace if they are not the admin
and there is at least one other member.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

Remove a member from an workspace.

Only workspace admins can remove members.
Admins cannot remove themselves.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

Validate workspace details using AI compliance check.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

Submit an appeal for workspace review after AI validation failure.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

body.name: reason
body.description: Detailed explanation of why this workspace should be approved. Minimum 50 characters.

operations.description:
Mark the AI onboarding as completed for this workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:write`

Get the current review status and appeal information for an workspace.

**Scopes**: `workspaces:read` `workspaces:write`
List OAuth2 clients belonging to the authenticated user.

params.name: limit
params.description: Size of a page, defaults to 10. Maximum is 100.

operations.description: Register a new OAuth2 client (RFC 7591).
Read an OAuth2 client's configuration (RFC 7592).
Update an OAuth2 client's configuration (RFC 7592).
Soft-delete an OAuth2 client (RFC 7592).
Begin the authorization code flow (returns consent metadata or auto-approves).
Handle the user's consent decision (allow or deny).
Exchange a grant for an access token.
Revoke an access or refresh token.
Return metadata about an active token.
Return OpenID Connect claims for the authenticated subject.
